title: Chip foundation — artefact_type slot enum + UUID wire + ArtefactTypeCatalogueProvider + Type/Status chip refactor + localStorage state
scope:
Replaces name-based artefact_type filtering with a project-locked slot enum + per-workspace UUID resolution. Adds an ArtefactTypeCatalogueProvider that fetches once per workspace and exposes useArtefactTypeBySlot for sidecar/page resolution. Migrates the work-items filter chip (Type / Status) to send UUIDs, enumerate from the catalogue, and persist state to localStorage keyed by workspace_id (per the URL-path-only rule).

Depends on PLA-0053 for workspace_id JWT claim + workspace-scoped artefacttypes service.

What PLA-0054 builds:

  1. DB: artefacts_types_slot TEXT NULL column with CHECK constraint (project-locked vocabulary: wrk_epic, wrk_story, wrk_defect, wrk_task, wrk_risk) + UNIQUE partial index per workspace.
  2. Backfill: assigns slots to the 5 system artefact types per workspace via name match (one-shot data migration; future system-type provisioning hands out slots at insert time).
  3. Backend: artefacttypes DTO includes slot; artefactitems handler accepts ?item_type_id=<uuid>[,<uuid>...] (multi-value, UUID-validated); ?item_type=<name> path dropped. Service Filters change from *string to []uuid.UUID; SQL emits WHERE artefact_type_id = ANY($N::uuid[]). Closes TD-FILTER-MULTI for the type filter.
  4. Frontend: ArtefactTypeCatalogueProvider context fetching /_site/artefact-types on mount, cached by workspace_id (via PLA-0053's useActiveWorkspace). Exposes useArtefactTypeBySlot(slot) hook (null-tolerant when slot not adopted) and useArtefactTypes() list hook.
  5. NavigationPie Type chip: TYPE_CHIP_OPTIONS literal deleted; options come from useArtefactTypes() sorted by artefacts_types_sort_order; chip onChange writes UUIDs; filter shape becomes string[] of UUIDs.
  6. Filter state moves off useSearchParams to localStorage, keyed filters:work-items:<workspace_id>. URL stays clean per the new URL-path-only rule.
  7. Sidecar: p_wizard_risks.json changes from "resourceUrl": "/work-items?item_type=risk" to "artefact_type_slot": "wrk_risk"; risk page resolves slot → UUID at mount and seeds the chip's default filter.
  8. Status chip becomes context-aware: when exactly one Type is selected, Status enumerates that type's flow_states; when none or multiple, Status falls back to the kind primitives.
  9. Empty-state UI: when the active workspace hasn't adopted a sidecar-referenced slot (e.g. no Risk type), the page renders a clear empty-state message instead of a 404.
  10. Tests: gadmin-rename invariance (rename Risk display name → chip still filters via UUID); catalogue null-tolerance; chip sends UUIDs; localStorage key scoping per workspace.
value:
Closes the chip's three structural bugs in one coherent change:

  • Chip refetches the tree — state moves off useSearchParams to localStorage; the chip's onChange directly drives tree refetches.
  • Gadmin rename doesn't break filters — UUID wire means the Risk type's display name can change to 'Issue' or 'Hazard' without breaking the chip or the sidecar.
  • Multi-select works end-to-end — backend accepts comma-joined UUIDs and emits = ANY($N::uuid[]); closes TD-FILTER-MULTI for the type filter.

Architecture wins:

  • Stable system-vs-display layer separation. Per the new feedback_system_vs_display_layers rule: UUIDs/slots are project-locked + invisible; catalogue names/labels are tenant-free + visible. The chip surfaces names; the system flows UUIDs.
  • Sidecar stays static + tenant-agnostic. Per feedback the sidecar carries the role/slot string; the page resolves to the per-workspace UUID at mount.
  • Foundation for strategic-layer chips. Future per-type pages (custom Bug page, Initiative page, Objective page) reuse the same catalogue + chip without code changes.
implementation_plan: RED-GREEN PROTOCOL (HARD RULE): every implementation story below is red-first. For each: (1) write the failing test described in the story's primary AC, (2) commit red with `test(<area>): red — <story-id>`, (3) implement, (4) commit green with `feat|fix|refactor(<area>): <description> [<story-id>]`. Red commits route through the `<rg>` substrate (PLA-0051) so they appear in Tracker's /red-green dashboard. Dedicated TEST stories (story 12 here) cover cross-cutting integration assertions not bound to a single implementation.

TRACKER REGRESSION LIBRARY: every test written by this plan is registered in Tracker under group `frontend-chip-foundation` and becomes a permanent regression check. Future plans' Tracker runs re-execute this group's tests to confirm no regression. Red AND green commits both POST to Tracker (via `<rg>` / `rg-runner`); the green POST closes the cycle and locks the test into the library. The exact `rg-runner` invocation should be verified against `cmd/rg-runner --help` in the MMFFDev - Tracker project before the first story in this plan ships (TD-RG-RUNNER-DOCS if undocumented).
Phase 0 — Schema substrate (stories 1, 2): slot column + CHECK + index + backfill.
Phase 1 — Backend DTO + wire (stories 3, 4, 5): catalogue includes slot; handler/service accept UUID; closes TD-FILTER-MULTI.
Phase 2 — Frontend catalogue context (story 6): provider + useArtefactTypeBySlot + useArtefactTypes.
Phase 3 — Chip refactor (stories 7, 8): catalogue-driven options; localStorage state.
Phase 4 — Sidecar + page integration (stories 9, 11): risk page uses slot reference; empty-state UI.
Phase 5 — Status chip + tests (stories 10, 12): context-aware Status; rename-invariance vitest.
areas_impacted: SQL: artefacts_types_slot column + unique partial index per workspace.
API: artefactitems handler + service Filters shape change to []uuid.UUID.
API: artefacttypes DTO extended with slot field.
UI: ArtefactTypeCatalogueProvider context + useArtefactTypeBySlot hook + NavigationPie chip refactor.
UI: WorkItemsFilters shape change (string[] UUIDs); localStorage migration off useSearchParams.
Sidecar: p_wizard_risks.json references slot string instead of resourceUrl with slug.
feature_list: artefacts_types_slot column + project-locked CHECK + unique partial index
NavigationPie Type chip catalogue-driven, UUID wire
Filter state in localStorage keyed by workspace_id
Status chip context-aware (per-type flow_states when one Type selected)
Empty-state UI when slot not adopted
features_extended: WorkItemsFilters — type field becomes UUID[]; filterQuery sends ?item_type_id=<uuids>.
NavigationPie — Type and Status chips consume catalogue providers.
features_removed: filters.type[0] single-value cap (replaced by full multi-value)

work_item_backlog.title: 00582 — SQL: artefacts_types_slot column + CHECK + unique partial index per workspace
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish a project-locked slot enum on artefact_types, so that the system can reference types stably across gadmin renames.

New migration adds artefacts_types_slot TEXT NULL with CHECK constraint enumerating the 5 system slots (wrk_epic, wrk_story, wrk_defect, wrk_task, wrk_risk). Unique partial index ensures one slot per workspace (NULL allowed for custom types). Append-only vocabulary; renames forbidden.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F3 — Slot enum substrate. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F3)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F3 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by CHECK constraint:** INSERT with slot='bogus' is rejected; slot='wrk_risk' is accepted; slot=NULL is accepted.
- **As Proven by unique partial index:** INSERT a second artefact_type with slot='wrk_risk' in the same workspace is rejected; INSERT in a different workspace succeeds.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00583 — SQL: backfill slots on existing 5 system artefact types per workspace
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish the 5 system artefact types backfilled with their slot values, so that the chip can resolve them on day one without UI churn.

Data migration walks each workspace's artefact_types and assigns slot values by name match: 'Epic' → wrk_epic, 'Story' → wrk_story, 'Defect' → wrk_defect, 'Task' → wrk_task, 'Risk' → wrk_risk. Custom-named system types in any workspace are surfaced as an audit warning, not silently skipped.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F3 — Slot enum substrate. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F3)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F3 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by idempotency:** re-running the migration is a no-op (already-assigned slots are not re-written).
- **As Proven by audit log:** any workspace whose Risk type has been renamed away from 'Risk' is logged with workspace_id + current type name for manual triage.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00584 — API: extend artefact_types DTO with slot field
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a frontend engineer, I wish the artefact_types DTO to expose slot, so that the catalogue context can resolve sidecar references at runtime.

ArtefactType Go struct + JSON response shape gains a slot field (nullable string). artefacttypes.Service queries select it; handler serialises it.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F3 — Slot enum substrate. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F3)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F3 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by handler response:** GET /_site/artefact-types returns a slot field on every row (null for custom types).
- **As Proven by unit test:** unmarshal a sample response and assert slot is populated for wrk_risk row and null for a custom Bug row.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00585 — API: rewrite artefactitems handler to accept ?item_type_id=<uuid>[,uuid...] (drop slug path)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish the artefactitems handler to filter by artefact_type UUID, so that gadmin renames don't break filters.

Handler accepts ?item_type_id=<uuid>[,<uuid>...] with comma-separated parsing + per-value UUID validation (400 on parse failure). The legacy ?item_type=<name> query param is removed entirely.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F4 — UUID wire end-to-end (closes TD-FILTER-MULTI). Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F4)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F4 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by handler test:** GET /_site/work-items?item_type_id=<uuid1>,<uuid2> returns the union (multi-value works).
- **As Proven by handler test:** GET /_site/work-items?item_type_id=not-a-uuid returns 400 with a clear error.
- **As Proven by handler test:** GET /_site/work-items?item_type=epic returns 400 (legacy path removed).

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00586 — API: artefactitems service Filters []uuid.UUID + WHERE ANY($1::uuid[]) (closes TD-FILTER-MULTI)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish artefactitems.Filters to carry multi-value UUID arrays, so that multi-select chips actually return matching rows.

Filters struct changes: ItemTypeIDs []uuid.UUID (was ItemType *string). SQL builder emits WHERE a.artefact_type_id = ANY($N::uuid[]). Closes the TD-FILTER-MULTI cap. Same shape applied to OwnerID (priority + status follow in PLA-0055 / a separate Status story).

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F4 — UUID wire end-to-end (closes TD-FILTER-MULTI). Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F4)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F4 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by SQL inspection:** ListWorkItems WHERE clause uses artefact_type_id = ANY($N::uuid[]).
- **As Proven by integration test:** ?item_type_id=<u1>,<u2> returns the union of both types' artefacts; no rows from a third type leak in.
- **As Proven by TD-FILTER-MULTI closure:** the register entry can be struck through with the resolution date.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00587 — UI: ArtefactTypeCatalogueProvider + useArtefactTypeBySlot + useArtefactTypes
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a frontend engineer, I wish a workspace-scoped artefact_type catalogue context, so that pages and chips can resolve slots to UUIDs without per-component fetches.

New ArtefactTypeCatalogueProvider mounts in app/(user)/layout.tsx. On mount + on workspace_id change (via useActiveWorkspace), fetches /_site/artefact-types and caches by workspace_id. Exposes useArtefactTypeBySlot(slot) and useArtefactTypes() hooks.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F5 — Catalogue + chip integration. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F5)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F5 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by cache test:** two components calling useArtefactTypes() trigger one fetch, not two.
- **As Proven by workspace-switch test:** when useActiveWorkspace returns a new workspace_id, the catalogue refetches and the hooks return the new workspace's types.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00588 — UI: NavigationPie Type chip enumerates from catalogue + sends UUIDs (drop TYPE_CHIP_OPTIONS literal)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a frontend engineer, I wish the Type chip to enumerate from the live catalogue, so that custom artefact types appear automatically and renames are reflected.

Deletes TYPE_CHIP_OPTIONS literal in work-items-tree-config.tsx. Chip's options prop comes from useArtefactTypes() filtered to scope='work' and sorted by artefacts_types_sort_order. WorkItemsFilters.type becomes UUID[]; chip onChange writes UUIDs.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F5 — Catalogue + chip integration. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F5)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F5 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by vitest:** NavigationPie Type chip renders one segment per artefact_type in the mocked catalogue, sorted by sort_order.
- **As Proven by vitest:** clicking a segment fires onChange with the artefact_type's UUID, not its slug.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00589 — UI: filter state in localStorage keyed by workspace_id (drop useSearchParams)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a frontend engineer, I wish filter state to live in localStorage scoped by workspace, so that the URL stays clean and workspace switches don't carry stale filters.

useWorkItemsFilters reworked: reads/writes localStorage key 'filters:work-items:<workspace_id>'. URL stays free of filter params. When workspace_id changes (workspace switch), the hook re-reads from the new key (effectively a per-workspace filter memory).

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F5 — Catalogue + chip integration. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F5)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F5 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by persistence test:** filter selection survives a page reload (localStorage rehydrate).
- **As Proven by workspace-switch test:** switching active workspace loads that workspace's filter state from its own localStorage key; the prior workspace's state is preserved.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00590 — Sidecar: p_wizard_risks.json references artefact_type_slot 'wrk_risk' + page resolves at mount
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a frontend engineer, I wish the risk sidecar to reference a project-locked slot, so that gadmin renames don't break the /risk page.

p_wizard_risks.json drops resourceUrl '/work-items?item_type=risk' and adds 'artefact_type_slot': 'wrk_risk'. /risk page mount reads the sidecar, resolves slot → UUID via useArtefactTypeBySlot, and seeds the chip's default filter with the resolved UUID.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F5 — Catalogue + chip integration. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F5)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F5 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by page mount test:** /risk page in a workspace with Risk adopted seeds the chip with the Risk UUID.
- **As Proven by rename test:** rename the Risk type to 'Issue' (display name only); /risk page still renders the same rows because the slot resolution is UUID-based.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00591 — UI: Status chip context-aware (per-type flow_states when one Type selected)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a user, I wish the Status chip to show my type's flow_states when I'm filtering one type, so that I see the actual states I can be in for that type.

Status chip enumeration depends on Type chip state: no Type or multiple Types selected → falls back to kind primitives (todo/in_progress/done/cancelled/accepted/in_review). Exactly one Type selected → enumerates that artefact_type's flow_states by UUID.

When Type changes invalidate the current Status selection (selected flow_state no longer exists in the new type's flow), Status auto-clears with a soft toast.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F6 — Status context-aware + rename invariance. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F6)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F6 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by vitest:** with one Type selected, Status chip enumerates that type's flow_states from the flowStatesApi.
- **As Proven by vitest:** changing Type from one with state X to one without state X clears the Status selection and emits a toast.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00592 — UI: empty-state when workspace hasn't adopted a sidecar-referenced slot
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a user, I wish a clear empty-state when my workspace doesn't have a referenced type adopted, so that I understand the page is not broken.

When a page mounts with a sidecar slot reference (e.g. wrk_risk) and the catalogue returns null for that slot, the page renders an in-place empty-state card explaining: 'This workspace doesn't have a [Risk] type configured. Ask your administrator to add one.' No 404, no crash, no blank grid.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F5 — Catalogue + chip integration. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F5)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F5 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by copy:** card text names the missing type (using the slot's display intent, e.g. 'Risk') and offers an actionable next step.
- **As Proven by no-crash:** page does not navigate to 404; the chip is hidden; no tree fetch is attempted.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00603 — TEST(F3): slot column + backfill + DTO surface test suite
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend/frontend engineer, I wish a single feature-level test suite covering feature F3 (Slot enum substrate), so that the suite becomes a permanent regression check in Tracker's library and meaningful end-to-end coverage replaces per-story plumbing tests.

**Covers stories:** 00582, 00583, 00584.

Single Go test suite asserting the slot enum substrate works end-to-end.

## Red-Green Protocol (HARD RULE)

1. **RED:** write failing suite. Fails on `main` (slot column doesn't exist). Commit: `test(artefacttypes): red — F3 slot substrate [00603]`. Register in Tracker under group `frontend-chip-foundation`, feature `F3`.
2. **GREEN:** stories 00582 → 00583 → 00584 land in order; suite passes after 00584.
3. **REGRESSION LOCK:** suite stays in Tracker library.

## Test scope

- Migration applied: artefacts_types_slot exists with CHECK + unique partial index.
- CHECK rejects slot='bogus'; accepts 'wrk_risk' and NULL.
- Unique partial index rejects duplicate slot per workspace; allows same slot across workspaces.
- After backfill: SELECT slot, COUNT(*) GROUP BY slot returns 5 rows × workspace_count.
- Backfill is idempotent (re-run is no-op).
- GET /_site/artefact-types returns slot field; populated for wrk_risk row, null for custom Bug.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by red commit:** suite committed and failing on `main`.
- **As Proven by green commit:** stories 00582–00584 land; suite green.
- **As Proven by regression entry:** suite locked into Tracker library.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00604 — TEST(F4): UUID-wire multi-value filter integration suite (closes TD-FILTER-MULTI)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend/frontend engineer, I wish a single feature-level test suite covering feature F4 (UUID wire end-to-end (closes TD-FILTER-MULTI)), so that the suite becomes a permanent regression check in Tracker's library and meaningful end-to-end coverage replaces per-story plumbing tests.

Single Go integration suite for the UUID wire + multi-value filter slice. Closes TD-FILTER-MULTI when this suite goes green.

## Red-Green Protocol (HARD RULE)

1. **RED:** write failing suite. Fails on `main` (handler still accepts ?item_type=epic and Filters is *string). Commit: `test(artefactitems): red — F4 UUID wire [00604]`. Register in Tracker under group `frontend-chip-foundation`, feature `F4`.
2. **GREEN:** stories 00585 → 00586 land; suite passes after 00586.

- ?item_type_id=not-a-uuid returns 400.
- ?item_type=epic returns 400 (legacy path removed).
- Filters.ItemTypeIDs is []uuid.UUID; *string field is gone (compile guard).
- SQL emits WHERE artefact_type_id = ANY($N::uuid[]).

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by red commit:** suite committed and failing on `main`.
- **As Proven by green commit:** stories 00585–00586 land; suite green; TD-FILTER-MULTI struck through.
- **As Proven by regression entry:** suite locked into Tracker library.

**Covers stories:** 00587, 00588, 00589, 00590, 00592.

Single vitest suite for the frontend catalogue + chip + localStorage + sidecar slice.

1. **RED:** write failing suite. Fails on `main` (no catalogue provider, chip reads TYPE_CHIP_OPTIONS, useSearchParams writes URL). Commit: `test(chip): red — F5 catalogue + chip [00605]`. Register in Tracker under group `frontend-chip-foundation`, feature `F5`.

3. **REGRESSION LOCK:** suite stays in Tracker library.

## Test scope

workspace switch: catalogue invalidates; new workspace types appear
chip onChange UUID matches workspace's actual artefact_type_id (not slug, not stale)

risks.risk: Status chip context-awareness introduces cross-chip dependency that's fragile under fast user toggling.
risks.mitigation: Story 10 explicitly tests the invalidate-on-Type-change path; toast emission gives the user a clear signal when their selection got cleared.

risks.risk: Backfill audit warning surfaces workspaces with non-standard system-type names; manual triage required before slot resolution is reliable for those workspaces.
risks.mitigation: Story 2 logs every audit case to dev_setup output; gadmin remediation is a one-row UPDATE per workspace.

risks.risk: localStorage key collision across multi-tab sessions (same workspace, two tabs) could create surprising filter sync.
risks.mitigation: Per-workspace key includes workspace_id; if a user wants per-tab independence later, sessionStorage migration is mechanical.
